changes.citation: orchestrator.py lines 369–379: create_analysis_context() calls user_data.update({...}) which mutates the user dict. Cursor must confirm in Phase 1 that moving this mutation earlier (before Step 1.6) does not affect any step between scoring_input_preparation (line 1067) and the current Step 2 position (line 1275). Assessment: the mutation adds questionnaire-derived keys only; simple_biomarkers computation at lines 1067–1069 does not read user dict. No blocker anticipated, but must be confirmed.

hardening_notes: Sprint may proceed to Stage 3 (kernel start) after branch creation and human review. Key implementation facts: (1) DEFECT LOCATION: orchestrator.py lines 1254–1267 (runtime_ctx from raw questionnaire_data) and lines 1272–1281 (create_analysis_context at Step 2 — too late). (2) TARGET MOVE: create_analysis_context() call must move to before Step 1.6. All required inputs (filtered_biomarkers, user, questionnaire_data) are available from line 1056 onward. (3) POST-CONTEXT CALL: replace build_runtime_context_snapshot(questionnaire_responses=questionnaire_data) with build_runtime_context_snapshot(questionnaire_responses=context.questionnaire_responses, lifestyle_factors=context.user.lifestyle_factors, medical_history=context.medical_history). Verify exact field names on AnalysisContext object. (4) PIPELINE_PHASE_ORDER: orchestrator_phases_v1.py lines 17–34 must be updated — move 'analysis_context' to before 'signal_evaluation'. (5) USER_DATA MUTATION: confirm in Phase 1 design note that user_data.update() at create_analysis_context() line 369 moving earlier does not affect any intermediate step. (6) REGRESSION: existing test_context_threading.py and test_runtime_context_evaluation.py must pass; new direct phase-order test must be added per Phase 5 requirements. (7) Do not merge without Claude audit, GPT architectural review, and explicit human approval.
